name: AJR
description:
is an American pop band founded by brothers {{nowrap|[[Adam Met|Adam]], Jack, and Ryan}} Met ({{Ne|Metzger}}), collectively a trio of vocalists, {{nowrap|multi-instrumentalists}}, and songwriters. In the 2010s and the 2020s, the cross-genre [[Trio (music)|trio]] of the 21st century in music has recorded [[List of songs recorded by AJR|over 100 songs]] and a live album and completed seven concert tours, music magazine [[Billboard (magazine)|''Billboard'']] ranked AJR as one of the top 100 best-selling touring artists of 2024.

Born in New York City, where the brothers spent their preteen years on [[busking]], [[tap dancing]], and singing covers, they started those in 2005 and slowly emerged to songwriting. AJR has released four [[indie pop]] [[extended plays]]: the polyonymous EP—''[[6foot1]]'' (2013) or the forward [[reissue]]—''I'm Ready'' EP (2014); ''[[Infinity (AJR EP)|Infinity]]'' (2014); and the polar EPs ''[[What Everyone's Thinking]]'' (2016), and ''[[What No One's Thinking]]'' (2025). The 2013–2014 polyonymous EP yielded their first pop radio platinum single "[[I'm Ready (AJR song)|I'm Ready]]".

At their home studio, AJR has written or co-written every song in their [[AJR discography#Studio albums|five-album discography]]: ''[[Living Room (AJR album)|Living Room]]'' (2015); ''[[The Click (album)|The Click]]'' (2017); the two [[Billboard 200|US ''Billboard'' 200]] top-ten albums ''[[Neotheater]]'' (2019), and ''[[OK Orchestra]]'' (2021); and ''[[The Maybe Man]]'' (2023). The 2017 [[electropop]] platinum album contained their first [[List of Billboard Alternative Songs number ones of the 2010s|''Billboard'' Alternative number-one song]] "[[Sober Up]]" (featuring [[Rivers Cuomo]]), and the 2021 [[orchestral pop]] gold album spawned—their first [[List of Billboard Hot 100 top-ten singles in 2021|Hot 100 top-ten single]] and also the [[Billboard Music Award for Top Rock Song#Bang!|''Billboard'' Music Award Top Rock Song]]—"[[Bang! (AJR song)|Bang!]]".

AJR is a {{nowrap|[[multi-platinum]]}} band for having eight platinum singles—including the three aforementioned songs; "[[Weak (AJR song)|Weak]]", "[[Burn the House Down (song)|Burn the House Down]]", "[[100 Bad Days]]", "[[Way Less Sad]]", and "[[World's Smallest Violin]]"—all these songs accounted for nineteen million [[Recording Industry Association of America#"Digital" single certification|certified units]] of their digital single sales in America.
